note: AC1-FR (x-bdf9): the Rust spawn gate (crates/fno-agents/src/spawn_gate.rs::slot_count) and the Python mirror (cli/src/fno/agents/spawn_gate.py::census().slot_count) must return the same worker-slot count for each scenario. Both test suites read THIS file. pid 'self' = the running test process (alive); pid 'dead' = a never-alive pid; an absent pid = a disk-only row. A registry row's optional 'claude_short_id' and a roster session's optional 'short' let a scenario force a roster<->registry short_id collision (the bg/adopted-worker case). Roster sessions must NOT count toward the slot cap; a live fno registry row must, even when it collides with a roster session.
